required_preconditions: The source packet remains accepted in the public mission review queue.
The source review remains valid, public, and linked to the same packet, mission, claim, and target level.
All cited public evidence remains accessible and privacy-safe.
The proposed level is strictly stronger than the current canonical claim level.
No new contradiction or material counterexample has appeared since review.
A maintainer has assigned or approved the canonical evidence-record identifier.
The change is implemented in a separate pull request and receives human review.
rollback_rules: Canonical promotions must be isolated in a dedicated pull request or squash commit.
Rollback uses a revert of that isolated commit rather than manual partial edits.
All source and generated files named by the plan must be restored together.
The accepted review and evidence packet remain preserved even when a promotion is reverted.
A rollback must record the superseding evidence or defect that caused the reversion.
publication_rules: A generated plan is an impact preview and implementation checklist, not an executable patch.
The planner must never write canonical claims, evidence, contradictions, missions, thresholds, release gates, tags, or releases.
Every plan requires a separate maintainer-reviewed pull request before canonical changes may occur.
Contradiction resolution is never implied by claim promotion and requires its own published criteria to pass.
The active campaign status must be regenerated after a canonical change; a plan may not advance the campaign lifecycle or declare a release ready.
Blocked plans remain public and may not be silently discarded.
